focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred struggles with car repairs while Barney, recently fired, boasts of invented fire and other ideas; Barney takes the car hood with his flamingo puppet to try fixing it himself.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Barney reveals he was fired from his job and responds by asserting his identity through claims of grand inventions, including fire, while using his flamingo puppet to take charge of fixing Fred's car.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: pursuing recognition for his ideas
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: determined to demonstrate his inventive abilities by attempting to fix Fred's car
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of financial stability or professional validation
evidence_quotes: BARNEY Ah, well, that appliance store and me, we kinda had a little adjustment --
FRED You got fired, didn't ya?
BARNEY I invented fire.
He uses the flamingo's jaws to snatch the car hood from Fred's surprised grasp.

relation_updates: defensive but friendly dynamic with Fred
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as a persistent dreamer despite setbacks

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Wilma and Betty, experienced professional shoppers, reunite with Mrs. Granite, laughing over their routine as they prepare for a shopping outing.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Barney constructs a replacement car hood using a mold and a mixture of raw materials, completing the process with a makeshift press.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: proving his inventions work
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred is visibly impressed by the successful creation of a functional car hood, despite initial skepticism.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the long-term durability and real-world performance of the hood remain untested.
evidence_quotes: Barney presses the car hood into some soft clay between two big blocks of stone.
He tosses the original car hood aside with a crash.
FRED (impressed) Two coo-coo berries? You made of money, Barney?
he yanks on a hanging rope and a giant rock weight slams down on the whole mess,

relation_updates: gains reluctant respect from Fred

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Feldspar catches the watch as Fred flees without a radar reading, prompting Feldspar to shout after him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Barney expresses pride in his invention but grows quiet when Fred pressures him to abandon his dreams for a quarry job, then briefly rekindles their shared past when they race down Lava Lane.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Barney considers whether to accept Fred's offer of stable work or continue pursuing his inventive ambitions
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: momentarily reconnected to his dreams through the go-cart memory, yet still uncertain about his future path
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Barney will pursue stability at the quarry or remain committed to his creative, unconventional lifestyle
evidence_quotes: BARNEY Not bad, eh? After it cures for an hour or so, it's solid as a rock.
BARNEY (wistful) Yeah, those were the days... we usta dream about coming back here some day with a real car and goin' for the goldrock...!
BARNEY Fred... I thought you said it was time to retire those old dreams.

relation_updates: Fred positions himself as Barney's mentor toward conventional success
status_updates: Barney's identity as a dreamer is challenged by Fred's authority
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as a loyal friend torn between practicality and his dreamer identity

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Slate arrives at a prehistoric children's school in style, unfazed by riding in the back with groceries, as Betty and Wilma follow.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Attempts to correct Fred's false claim about inventing Fibrerock but is silenced and pressured into complicity.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain honesty while supporting Fred
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: becomes an unwilling participant in Fred's fabrication
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his contribution is uncredited and his truthfulness ignored
evidence_quotes: That's a lie, Mr. Slate!
Well, actually, Fred, it was the other way ar--
SLATE (pushing Barney aside) Flintstone, I have just one thing to say to you --
Sure, Fred, right, right.

relation_updates: compromised loyalty to Fred (goes along with lie)
disregarded by Mr. Slate despite speaking up
status_updates: presented as co-creator of Fibrerock, though unwillingly
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as the silent truth-bearer in a culture of exaggeration

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Barney downplays their effort in securing the deal, calling it accidental, while Fred remains excited to share the news with their partners, focusing on the positive outcome.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Barney acknowledges receiving a financial stake in Fibrerock but emphasizes that the outcome resulted from chance, not effort.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resist Fred's framing of the success as earned through hard work
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Barney transitions from unemployment to part-owner of Fibrerock with a 0.5% profit share
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: disagreement with Fred about whether success stems from effort or luck

relation_updates: reinforces honest dynamic with Fred
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as the humble beneficiary of unintended consequences

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Barney alerts Fred that the Buffaloes, led by Piltdown, are heading to the quarry to sabotage the assembly line, risking arrest; Fred agrees they must stop them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: moral conscience and initiator of joint action
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Recognizes the illegal nature of the Buffaloes' plan to sabotage the assembly line and urgently convinces Fred to intervene.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prevent the Buffaloes from destroying the assembly line and facing arrest
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: successfully persuades Fred to act, reestablishing their partnership and initiating a joint effort to stop the group
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between workers' frustrations and the need to uphold the law remains unresolved
evidence_quotes: only one member has avoided the herding instinct: Barney.
Then Barney runs up, shakes him by the shoulders.
No, Fred, if they do that they'll be breakin' the law! Our brother Buffalo could go to jail!

relation_updates: reestablishes partnership with Fred after brief rift
status_updates: acts as sole dissenter from group impulse
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as the voice of reason in group conflict

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lava dismisses Barney's concern over altered Fibrerock taste, sending him on an unexpected vacation despite Barney's nine-day tenure and pending safety checks before the audit.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: employee raising a safety concern about materials
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reports a change in the taste of Fibrerock foam, suggesting possible contamination, but is abruptly sent on vacation by Lava instead of being investigated
timeline_nodes.goal_state: conduct tests to verify Fibrerock composition
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: removed from duty and excluded from production work without investigation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential issue with Fibrerock mix remains unexamined
evidence_quotes: Excuse me, Mister Lava -- ? I think you oughta know... something could be wrong with the Fibrerock mix.
It tastes different.
Rubble, Rubble! We're fighting a deadline to finish Slate City... and we're not pulling the plug on production to satisfy your tastebuds!
I'm authorizing a nice vacation for you, Rubble. Starting tomorrow.

relation_updates: Lava distances from Barney under guise of concern
status_updates: Barney is removed from production duties
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as a conscientious worker dismissed despite valid concern

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Barney identifies coo-coo berry sap as critical to prevent real Slate City's collapse; he and Fred rush to save it using the sap, as the model city crumbles completely.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Identifies the absence of coo-coo berry sap as the cause of structural failure in the model, links it to real-world danger, and takes immediate action to address it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prevent real-world collapse of Slate City
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: prepares and deploys with Fred to deliver coo-coo berry sap as an emergency fix for the failing city structure
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the coo-coo berry sap will successfully stabilize the real Slate City remains unknown
evidence_quotes: Suddenly Barney gets it. He looks from the older models to the now decomposing little Fibresphere with growing awareness.
Remember I said the Fibrefoam tasted funny? It was the coo-coo sap that was missing!
Barney runs to a locker, grabs a big plunger-powered flit gun, begins filling it with a red liquid. BARNEY Coo-coo berry sap, Fred!
Barney gives it a quick test squirt. Fred nods, and they rush out of the room.

relation_updates: acts as equal partner with Fred in crisis
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as the key diagnostician in a systemic failure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred and Barney escape their crashed car, relieved the flit gun is intact; hearing a siren, they split up to avoid capture, aiming to reach the Slate Needle.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After escaping a car crash, Barney confirms the flit gun is undamaged and proposes splitting up to evade capture and reach the Slate Needle.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reach the Slate Needle to prevent disaster
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred and Barney are separated and on the run, mission still active but under greater risk
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: pursued by police; Fred and Barney are now separated
evidence_quotes: Fred and Barney stagger out of the wreck.
Barney, covered with debris, rummages around and sighs with relief when he finds the flit gun of coo-coo sap is still intact.
BARNEY (hearing a siren) We'd better split up; that way at least one of us can make it to the Slate Needle -- !
then head off in two directions.

relation_updates: mutual trust with Fred reaffirmed through hand-slap
status_updates: still in possession of flit gun
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as a resilient operator in high-stakes evasion

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The pythonsaurus admires Fred's costume, feels dejected upon recognizing him, and follows the group to safety as Slate Needle collapses behind them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Barney creates a safe path through the collapsing structure using his flit gun and directs the group's evacuation to safety.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: safely evacuate all civilians
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Barney and the group are relocated to the adjacent Fibresphere after escaping the collapse.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: long-term consequences of the collapse and fraud remain unresolved
evidence_quotes: BARNEY Come on, everybody -- we'll go out the way we came!
Barney squirts a pathway with his flit gun, solidifying a route in the crumbling Fibrerock.
Then Barney and Fred stand back nobly, wave everyone on into the adjacent Fibresphere.
Fred and Barney cross over into the Fibresphere.

relation_updates: guides group as a leader figure
status_updates: moves from danger to relative safety
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as an emergent leader in disaster response

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fibrerock, now edible, is devoured by dinosaurs and Dino; Barney realizes it's transformed into Dino kibble, making Fred and Barney rich, causing Slate to faint as the camera pulls back.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Recognizes that the altered Fibrerock is edible and identical to Dino kibble, leading to the realization that he and Fred now possess a valuable, marketable resource.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understand the nature of the transformed Fibrerock
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: gains worldwide rights to Fibrerock along with Fred, transitioning from employee to co-owner of a commercially viable product
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential future legal liabilities associated with Fibrerock use
evidence_quotes: FRED Barney, we -- we're rich! Yabba dabba do -- !
SLATE Your only obligation will be to cover oh, whatever legal expenses might surface at some later time...

relation_updates: Slate shifts from exploiter to grantor of rights
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Barney as the observant partner who identifies the practical value of the discovery

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Barney Rubble begins as a supportive neighbor and inventive dreamer, initially unemployed but creatively driven. He proves his ingenuity by crafting a functional car hood, only to face pressure from Fred to conform to traditional work. Despite being sidelined and uncredited, Barney remains loyal, eventually emerging as a moral and practical leader during crises. By the story's end, his technical insight and courage directly save lives and reveal a new source of wealth, transitioning him from overlooked tinkerer to co-owner of a transformative invention.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A Rube Goldberg machine on a Bedrock lawn activates spinning woolly mammoths that spray water, one complaining of aching sinuses, blending prehistoric elements with automated suburban routines.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred finishes his workday at the quarry and returns home, where he gathers his family and neighbors for a trip to the drive-in theater.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: return home and gather family for leisure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred is at the drive-in theater with his family and friends, beginning a shared recreational outing.
evidence_quotes: Fred SKIDS to a halt in the driveway of his three-bedroom ranch cave.
HONKS his HORN... BARNEY, BETTY and BAM-BAM come out of the neighboring house, hop in!
Fred and company pull in. He takes a giant sea shell \"speaker\" off of its wooden stand and hooks it to his car door.

relation_updates: reaffirms close bond with Wilma and Pebbles
confirms cooperative friendship with Barney and Betty

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred's morning is disrupted by Dino's playful antics, causing chaos as the radio reports on Bedrock's kibble crisis threatening the animal-powered civilization.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred's attempt to start his morning is interrupted by Dino's energetic behavior and news of a citywide kibble shortage, disrupting his sense of control.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: wake up and begin morning routine
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred is physically overwhelmed by Dino and made aware of a threat to the city's animal-dependent systems
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the kibble shortage may disrupt pet care and broader societal functions in Bedrock
evidence_quotes: Dino, cut it out -- !
By now Dino's affection attack has knocked both master and pet to the floor in a jumble of bedclothes, asses and elbows.
In the news, Bedrock civic and business leaders met yesterday to discuss the growing kibble crisis.

relation_updates: shows affectionate but strained dynamic with Dino
status_updates: learns of growing kibble shortage affecting city

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred struggles with car repairs while Barney, recently fired, boasts of invented fire and other ideas; Barney takes the car hood with his flamingo puppet to try fixing it himself.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred's car is damaged and then taken by Barney, who uses a flamingo puppet to seize the hood, undermining Fred's attempt to repair it.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resume breakfast and restore normalcy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred is left helpless as his car is further damaged and taken out of his control, contributing to his mounting frustration.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Barney's unemployment and erratic behavior continue to disrupt Fred's attempts to maintain order.

He uses the flamingo's jaws to snatch the car hood from Fred's surprised grasp.

relation_updates: shares darkly comic moment with Wilma about misfortune

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Wilma and Betty, experienced professional shoppers, reunite with Mrs. Granite, laughing over their routine as they prepare for a shopping outing.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred witnesses Barney construct a functional car hood from unusual materials, challenging his initial doubt.

evidence_quotes: Fred watches, skeptical, as Barney presses the car hood into some soft clay between two big blocks of stone.
FRED (impressed) Two coo-coo berries? You made of money, Barney?
He stops as Barney cranks up the weight on a ratchet, proudly lifts out a perfect (though greenish) duplicate of the car hood.
As Fred examines it, impressed despite himself,

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred proudly shows Barney his influence at Slate Construction, but scowls upon seeing his weight on the truck scale, then leads Barney into the quarry as the camera shifts to the office window.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred demonstrates his authority and social standing at work while briefly reacting with discomfort to his weight reading on the scale.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure a job for Barney and affirm his own importance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred appears confident and in control at work but shows a moment of personal discomfort regarding his weight.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: personal insecurity about physical appearance
evidence_quotes: Fred acknowledges these, beaming in the respect he gets here.
knocks an offending speck from his parking sign (F. Flintstone - Shop Steward).
Fred steps towards the quarry, admires the activity, hands on hips, master of all he surveys.
Fred is standing on it and it's pushing 250. With a scowl, Fred grabs Barney's sleeve and they go into the quarry.

relation_updates: reinforces positive rapport with workers and Shirley

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Slate arrives at a prehistoric children's school in style, unfazed by riding in the back with groceries, as Betty and Wilma follow.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred takes credit for creating the material from his car hood when accused of causing the office collapse, and is praised instead of punished.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid blame and gain approval
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: recognized as the inventor of a new building material and pulled aside for private discussion with Mr. Slate

Mr. Slate -- Mr. Slate -- are you all right?
Well, Mr. Slate, I call it -- (hesitating, sotto) -- Barney, what do I call it?

Fred relies on Barney to maintain false narrative

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Barney downplays their effort in securing the deal, calling it accidental, while Fred remains excited to share the news with their partners, focusing on the positive outcome.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred attributes their success to hard work and personal virtue, rejecting Barney's claim that it was accidental.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain belief in meritocratic effort
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred reaffirms a narrative of earned success despite Barney's correction
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: disagreement about the role of luck versus effort in their outcome

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred is credited for Fibrerock's success, but workers are shocked when Slate announces layoffs, replacing adults with children; Fred, blamed by coworkers, resolves to take action.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred is publicly credited for Fibrerock's success just before layoffs are announced, leading coworkers to blame him for their job losses.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Respond to the workers' backlash and address his role in the layoffs triggered by Fibrerock's adoption.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred resolves to take action after being confronted by coworkers who hold him responsible for the job cuts.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fred must navigate the conflict between his association with the innovation and his loyalty to fellow workers who are losing their jobs.
evidence_quotes: thanks to your very own fellow worker, Fred Flintstone!

WORKER # Thanks to Fred Flintstone?
WORKER # Flintstone! What kinda shop steward are you?

relation_updates: Workers initially praise Fred, then turn on him with blame
status_updates: Fred is publicly associated with both innovation and job losses

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred returns silently after meeting management, fails to speak, hides in his locker, and sobs, leaving workers realizing he did not secure their demands.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Fred returns from a failed meeting with management, stammers incoherently, then retreats into his locker and sobs, showing emotional collapse.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred is emotionally broken and isolated; workers realize he failed to secure their demands.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fred must recover from personal defeat and regain credibility with the workers.
evidence_quotes: Fred staggers over to his locker, opens it... gets in, and closes the door.
We hear a MUFFLED SOB.
FRED Akkk... something... noble... man ... job... nose... wheel... shoulder... grindstone... hommm... yggggg...
All the Workers watch expectantly as Fred comes out.

relation_updates: Workers lose trust in Fred
status_updates: Fred is isolated and emotionally defeated

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Wilma, Betty, the kids, and Dino watch a fashion show featuring Fibrerock clothing replacing fur, followed by a musical display of Fibrerock props and a choir of mechanical animals singing 'It's a Fibre World.'

timeline_nodes.goal_state: release guilt and seek forgiveness
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally vulnerable but reconciled with Barney
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Fred will sustain humility after the crisis passes

timeline_nodes.goal_state: to deliver coo-coo berry sap to prevent the real Slate City from collapsing

... Is gonna happen for real in the real Slate City!

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fibrerock, now edible, is devoured by dinosaurs and Dino; Barney realizes it's transformed into Dino kibble, making Fred and Barney rich, causing Slate to faint as the camera pulls back.

timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: potential legal liabilities from the destruction caused during the transformation
evidence_quotes: I'm going to give you back all world-wide rights to Fibrerock, free and clear.
FRED (mouth full) Dhinough khibble!!
Your only obligation will be to cover oh, whatever legal expenses might surface at some later time...

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Fred begins as a dependable family man and quarry worker, navigating daily chaos with resilience. His journey shifts from personal insecurity and opportunism to moral reckoning, as he rises and falls in status due to a fabricated invention. After betraying Barney and losing trust, Fred confronts his failures, reconciles, and ultimately acts with genuine courage and loyalty. The story culminates in accidental fortune and restored relationships, though his domestic struggles persist.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lava warns Slate of impending bankruptcy, causing a furious reaction; Slate rejects familial terms and demands direct, actionable reports, emphasizing his authority.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reacting with anger to news of impending bankruptcy, Mr. Slate rejects familial address and demands direct reporting, reinforcing professional hierarchy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain control and authority in business operations
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: visibly shocked and destabilized after learning of imminent bankruptcy
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: company faces imminent bankruptcy
evidence_quotes: how'd you like my fist to intersect with your nose? I've told you a hundred times, don't call me uncle in the office!
Now, I didn't arrange your scholarship to Harvrock University so you could waste my time!
So stop beating around the bushasaurus and get to the bottom slime!
LAVA: You'll be bankrupt in six months. Slate does a Danny Thomas spit take all over the model.

relation_updates: reinforces professional boundary with Jerry Lava by rejecting 'uncle' title
asserts hierarchical superiority over subordinate

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A crane reassembly at the quarry goes awry when the wrecking ball breaks loose, rolling down and destroying everything in its path, endangering workers and animals.

timeline_nodes.goal_state: preserve development deal and avoid bankruptcy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Slate arrives at a prehistoric children's school in style, unfazed by riding in the back with groceries, as Betty and Wilma follow.

timeline_nodes.goal_state: identify cause of accident and restore control
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: shifts from assigning blame to pursuing innovation, focusing on the car hood's potential for construction
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: true cause of accident obscured by false accusation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Slate arrives at a prehistoric children's school in style, unfazed by riding in the back with groceries, as Betty and Wilma follow.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Adopts 'Fibrerock' as a new construction material, publicly endorses Fred and Barney, and dismisses his advisor Lava's expertise.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: capitalize on new material to save the business
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: enthusiastic and committed to shifting to Fibrerock
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension with Lava and potential resistance to the new material

auxiliary.persona_anchor: pragmatic leader swayed by tangible results

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fred is credited for Fibrerock's success, but workers are shocked when Slate announces layoffs, replacing adults with children; Fred, blamed by coworkers, resolves to take action.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Announces layoffs of 22 adult workers to be replaced by children, citing Fibrerock's lightness, while publicly crediting Fred Flintstone and triggering worker backlash against him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: increase profits and efficiency through cheaper labor
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Workforce is shocked and demoralized; Fred is seen by coworkers as responsible for the layoffs and becomes isolated.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fred must respond to being blamed for the layoffs and the injustice faced by his coworkers.
evidence_quotes: thanks to your very own fellow worker, Fred Flintstone!
Flintstone! What kinda shop steward are you?
Your pink slips will be in your final pay envelopes along with a ticket to the grand opening of Slate City.

relation_updates: workers now view Fred as responsible for job loss

status_updates: enacts cost-cutting measures using Fibrerock's lightness

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Slate mocks firing employees, then uses a joke to pressure Fred into leaving after criticizing his PR for Fibrerock, threatening his job to make him quit.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: boss using psychological pressure to enforce compliance
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Slate uses mockery and veiled threats to pressure Fred into leaving the office after criticizing his handling of Fibrerock's public relations.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: remove Fred from the PR role without formal dismissal
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fred exits the office in silence, complying with Slate's demand
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fred's loyalty is questioned, but no formal action is taken against him
evidence_quotes: SLATE (furious) Flintstone!
I'm getting a bad feeling about Fibrerock. A bad feeling about... you.
Then turn around and walk out of here and we'll forget all about this.
Fred swallows, looks at Lava, goes meekly out.

relation_updates: undermines Fred's position and confidence
reinforces Lava as trusted ally
status_updates: maintains control over Fibrerock's public image

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lava distracts security guards Piltdown and Pyrite with a golf trophy; Barney tries to steal Fibrerock foam but is caught, ejected; guards laugh as Lava turns attention to dealing with Slate.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Proposes and secures naming rights for two major structures in the model city, 'Slate Scrapers' and 'Slate Needle', as personal legacies.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: assert identity and legacy through naming
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally absorbed in the model city, viewing it as a personal achievement
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: signs cost overrun documents without reading them, enabling Lava's undisclosed plans
evidence_quotes: SLATE (modestly) How about, 'Slate Needle'?
LAVA 'Slate Need...' (astonished) ... How do you keep coming up with these? It's uncanny.

relation_updates: Lava defers to Slate's input with admiration
status_updates: gains symbolic recognition as namesake of major structures

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lava confesses to stealing coo-coo berries to cover stock losses, admitting the sap is unnecessary; Slate attacks him, but structural collapse interrupts the fight, endangering all.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Discovers Lava's theft of coo-coo berries and sabotage of Fibrerock formula, reacts with violent intent, but is interrupted by structural collapse.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect the integrity of Fibrerock and the Slate family reputation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically confronts Lava in rage after betrayal, then shifts focus to immediate survival as building collapses
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to address the structural instability threatening the city
evidence_quotes: SLATE Of course it is! Why, without the coo-coo berry sap, Fibrerock would be completely -- (realizing) -- unstable...
SLATE I'll tell you what's superfluous, Lava... your liver! So why don't we remove it -- ! He leaps, snarling, on top of Lava...
Fred and Barney pull them apart.

relation_updates: relationship with Lava collapses into hostility

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The pythonsaurus admires Fred's costume, feels dejected upon recognizing him, and follows the group to safety as Slate Needle collapses behind them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Retrieves the severed head of his statue as the only item saved from the collapse of Slate Needle.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: holding a physical remnant of former status
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: emotional response to collapse deferred
evidence_quotes: Slate picks up the head of the statue, his only souvenir.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fibrerock, now edible, is devoured by dinosaurs and Dino; Barney realizes it's transformed into Dino kibble, making Fred and Barney rich, causing Slate to faint as the camera pulls back.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Acknowledges past mistreatment of Fred and Barney, and transfers full worldwide rights to Fibrerock as a gesture of gratitude after they save him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: attempting to make amends after being saved by those he mistreated
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: no longer owns Fibrerock; expresses guilt and grants Fred and Barney full rights to the material
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: underestimates potential future liabilities despite visible destruction around him
evidence_quotes: I treated you horribly... I ignored you, abused you... and then you come here and risk your necks like this...
I'm going to give you back all world-wide rights to Fibrerock, free and clear.
Your only obligation will be to cover oh, whatever legal expenses might surface at some later time...
Oh, you never know...

relation_updates: shifts from adversarial to grateful toward Fred and Barney
status_updates: no longer sole owner of Fibrerock
auxiliary.persona_anchor: humbled and visibly shaken, showing remorse and reluctant generosity

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Mr. Slate begins as a volatile, authority-driven businessman facing financial collapse, but pivots to visionary leadership upon discovering Fibrerock. His desperation for control leads to exploitative labor decisions and familial betrayal, culminating in the physical and symbolic destruction of his legacy. Ultimately, he acknowledges his moral failings, relinquishes control of Fibrerock to Fred and Barney, and transitions from antagonist to a humbled benefactor.
